title: Crimson Empire (audio drama)
content:
{{Top|rwm|new|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the audio dramatization|the [[Star Wars: Crimson Empire|comic series]] or the [[Crimson_Empire|government]]}} {{Audiobook |image=[[File:CrimsonEmpire-audiodrama.jpg]] |title=''Crimson Empire'' |author=*[[Mike Richardson]]'''{{HighBridge|url=starwarscrimsonempire.html|text=''Star Wars: Crimson Empire''}} *[[Randy Stradley]] |narrator= |producer=[[Tom Voegeli]] |director=[[Peter Moore]] |cover artist=[[Dave Dorman]]''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire]]'' |illustrator= |publisher=[[HighBridge Audio]] |release date=[[March 2]], [[1999]] |rereleased= |media type=*Compact disc *Cassette tape{{Amazon|domain=sg|url=Star-Wars-Crimson-Ensemble-Cast/dp/1565112989|text=Star Wars: Crimson Empire Audio Cassette – Audiobook}} |run time=1 hour 23 minutes
(83 minutes) |discs=2 |cassettes=2 |pages= |isbn=9781565119710 |timeline=[[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]]The ''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' places the [[Star Wars: Crimson Empire|comic series]] the audio drama is based on to [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]] |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} ''Crimson Empire'' was a full cast audio dramatization of ''[[Star Wars: Crimson Empire|comic series of the same name]]'' originally published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]. The audio drama was released by [[HighBridge Audio]] as a two compact disc set and a two cassette tape set on [[March 2]], [[1999]]; one year after the comic series ended. It was directed by [[Peter Moore]] and produced by [[Tom Voegeli]] and featured many of the same voice artists as the [[Star Wars: Dark Forces (novellas)|''Dark Forces'' audio dramas]] that were released the previous few years. ==Publisher's summary== '''The explosive ''Star Wars'' saga continues! ''Crimson Empire'' introduces you to a new generation of space heroes and thrilling adventures!''' As the story opens, the Empire is in turmoil; Luke Skywalker has destroyed the last of Emperor Palpatine's clones, and the Dark Lord Carnor Jax plans to seize the throne. But he has a formidable opponent to contend with: the brave Kir Kanos. The only survivor of the elite Crimson Guard, he alone stands between Jax and universal domination. On the run and fighting for his life in a world of disloyalty and deceit, Kanos takes refuge on the planet Phaeda, joining forces with the fierce, beautiful New Republic leader Mirith Sinn and her underground warriors. Soon, the Empire holds Mirith's band under siege, and none other than Kanos comes to their rescue. Why is he fighting with them? Is he on their side? Finally, the stage is set for the clash between the last of the Royal Guard: Kir Kanos vs. Carnor Jax. Only one can walk away. Who dies? What happens to the New Republic? Listen and find out as the suspense and action of this galactic story reaches its compelling conclusion to the first part of the '''''Crimson Empire''''' series. ==Plot summary== {{Plot|adaptation}} ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|soundcat=Audio files from Crimson Empire (audio)}} ===Editions=== *{{ISBN|9781565112988}}; [[March 2]], [[1999]]; [[HighBridge Audio]]; Cassette Tape *{{ISBN|9781565119710}}; March 2, 1999; HighBridge Audio; Compact disc ===Cover gallery=== This is a gallery of the different cover variations of ''Crimson Empire (audio)''. Cutler]]''' *'''[[Christopher Denton]]''' *'''[[Matt Guidry]]''' *'''[[Allen Hamilton]]''' *'''[[Jeremy Jackson]]''' *'''[[Johar Husain]]''' *'''[[Tom Keith]]''' *'''[[Michael Paul Levin]]''' *'''[[Bill McCallum]]''' *'''[[Peter Moore]]''' *'''[[Cliff Rakerd]]''' *'''[[John Wehrman]]''' |crew= *Written by '''[[Mike Richardson]]''' and '''[[Randy Stradley]]''' *Produced by '''[[Tom Voegeli]]''' *Directed by '''[[Peter Moore]]''' *Music composed by '''[[John Williams]]''' *Dialogue recorded by '''[[Tom Mudge]]''' and '''[[Craig Thorson]]''' *Production assistance by '''[[Michelle Winter]]''' *Cover art by '''[[Dave Dorman]]''' *Original Dark Horse Comics jacket design adapted by '''[[Mark Simoson]]''' }} ==Appearances== {{IncompleteApp}} {{App |characters= |organisms= |droids= |events= |locations= |organizations= |species= |vehicles= |technology= |miscellanea= }} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{HighBridge|url=starwarscrimsonempire.html|text=''Star Wars: Crimson Empire''}} {{AudioDramasNav}} {{Interlang |ka=ალისფერი იმპერია (აუდიოსპექტაკლი) }} [[Category:Legends audio dramas]]
